item:
Concept: “archived (bookmark boolean property)”
Statement: Whenever someone or something is archived, an archiving happened — something took place in time by which it was put away for keeping.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:archived

item:
Concept: “begin (happening X begins at time T)”
Statement: In every case of “begin (happening X begins at time T)” as described above, X is something that happens or goes on in time (a happening or process) — not a person, a place, or a time.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:begin

item:
Concept: “begin (happening X begins at time T)”
Statement: In every case of “begin (happening X begins at time T)” as described above, T is a time — a moment or stretch of time.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:begin

item:
Concept: “believe (X believes Y)”
Statement: In every case of “believe (X believes Y)” as described above, X is someone or something that exists on its own and lasts through time — not a time, a place, or a mere happening.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:believe

item:
Concept: “believe (X believes Y)”
Statement: In every case of “believe (X believes Y)” as described above, Y is something that can be thought — a content, the kind of thing that could also be put into words.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:believe

item:
Concept: “birth (the event)”
Statement: Every birth is a happening — something that takes place in time (an event), not a lasting object.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:birth

item:
Concept: “birth (the event)”
Statement: In every case of a birth, it happens at some time — there is a time at which the birth takes place.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:birth

item:
Concept: “bookmark”
Statement: Every bookmark is a kept mark or record — something that stands for something else so that someone can come back to it; it is not itself a happening or a time.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:bookmark

item:
Concept: “bookmark”
Statement: In every case of a bookmark, it was made — a making or saving of it happened at some time.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:bookmark

item:
Concept: “bookmark”
Statement: In every case of a bookmark, there is something the bookmark marks or points to — something other than the bookmark itself.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:bookmark

item:
Concept: “break (X breaks Y)”
Statement: In every case of “break (X breaks Y)” as described above, X is what makes the breaking happen — someone or something.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:break

item:
Concept: “break (X breaks Y)”
Statement: In every case of “break (X breaks Y)” as described above, Y is a thing that can be in one piece and then not — something with a body or material to it, not a time and not a bare idea.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:break

item:
Concept: “break (X breaks Y)”
Statement: In every case of “break (X breaks Y)” as described above, the breaking happens at some time — there is a time at which it takes place.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:break

item:
Concept: “broken”
Statement: Whenever someone or something is broken, being broken is a way that thing now is — a condition of it, not something separate from it.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:broken

item:
Concept: “cause (happening X causes happening Y)”
Statement: In every case of “cause (happening X causes happening Y)” as described above, X is something that happens or is the case — a happening or situation, not a lasting object, a place, or a time.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:cause

item:
Concept: “cause (happening X causes happening Y)”
Statement: In every case of “cause (happening X causes happening Y)” as described above, Y is something that happens or comes about — a happening or outcome, not a lasting object, a place, or a time.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:cause

item:
Concept: “cause (happening X causes happening Y)”
Statement: In every case of “cause (happening X causes happening Y)” as described above, the causing happens at some time — there is a time at which it takes place.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:cause

item:
Concept: “celebration”
Statement: Every celebration is a happening — something people do that takes place in time (an event).
subject: urn:kernel-v0:celebration

item:
Concept: “celebration”
Statement: In every case of a celebration, something happens — there are things done as part of it.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:celebration

item:
Concept: “change (the event)”
Statement: In every case of a change, there is something that changes — something that was one way before, and another way after.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:change

item:
Concept: “change (the event)”
Statement: In every case of a change, it happens at some time — there is a time at which the change takes place.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:change

item:
Concept: “condolence (the words)”
Statement: Every condolence is something said — words, spoken or written, offered to someone.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:condolence

item:
Concept: “condolence (the words)”
Statement: In every case of a condolence, the words are offered to someone — there is one the condolence is for.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:condolence

item:
Concept: “condolence (the words)”
Statement: In every case of a condolence, someone offers the words — there is a speaker or writer of them.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:condolence

item:
Concept: “conversation”
Statement: Every conversation is a happening — an exchange that takes place in time (an event).
subject: urn:kernel-v0:conversation

item:
Concept: “conversation”
Statement: In every case of a conversation, words are said to someone — those speaking are speaking to someone.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:conversation

item:
Concept: “conversation”
Statement: In every case of a conversation, someone says things — there is at least one person speaking.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:conversation

item:
Concept: “death (the event)”
Statement: Every death is a happening — something that takes place in time (an event).
subject: urn:kernel-v0:death

item:
Concept: “death (the event)”
Statement: In every case of a death, something living dies — there is one whose death it is.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:death

item:
Concept: “death (the event)”
Statement: In every case of a death, it happens at some time — there is a time at which the death takes place.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:death

item:
Concept: “end (happening X ends at time T)”
Statement: In every case of “end (happening X ends at time T)” as described above, X is something that happens or goes on in time (a happening or process) — not a person, a place, or a time.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:end

item:
Concept: “end (happening X ends at time T)”
Statement: In every case of “end (happening X ends at time T)” as described above, T is a time — a moment or stretch of time.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:end

item:
Concept: “event”
Statement: In every case of a event, it happens at some time — there is a time at which the event takes place.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:event

item:
Concept: “find (X finds Y)”
Statement: In every case of “find (X finds Y)” as described above, X is the one that does the finding — someone or something that can look for things or come upon them; not a time or a place.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:find

item:
Concept: “find (X finds Y)”
Statement: In every case of “find (X finds Y)” as described above, Y is what is found — something that was not in view, or not known to be where it is, and then is.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:find

item:
Concept: “forget (X forgets Y)”
Statement: In every case of “forget (X forgets Y)” as described above, X is the one who forgets — someone, or some being, that could know or remember things; not a time, a place, or a mere thing.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:forget

item:
Concept: “forget (X forgets Y)”
Statement: In every case of “forget (X forgets Y)” as described above, Y is what is forgotten — something that was known, remembered, or meant to be done.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:forget

item:
Concept: “friend (X is a friend of Y)”
Statement: In every case of “friend (X is a friend of Y)” as described above, X is a being one can have a bond with — someone, not a mere thing, a time, or a place.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:friend

item:
Concept: “friend (X is a friend of Y)”
Statement: In every case of “friend (X is a friend of Y)” as described above, Y is a being one can have a bond with — someone, not a mere thing, a time, or a place.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:friend

item:
Concept: “gift”
Statement: In every case of a gift, there is someone the gift is for — one meant to have it.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:gift

item:
Concept: “gift”
Statement: In every case of a gift, a giving is in play — the gift is given, or meant to be given.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:gift

item:
Concept: “give (X gives Y to someone)”
Statement: In every case of “give (X gives Y to someone)” as described above, X is the giver — the one from whom Y goes; someone or something that can have and hand over.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:give

item:
Concept: “give (X gives Y to someone)”
Statement: In every case of “give (X gives Y to someone)” as described above, Y is what is given — something that can go from one to another.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:give

item:
Concept: “give (X gives Y to someone)”
Statement: In every case of “give (X gives Y to someone)” as described above, the giving happens at some time — there is a time at which it takes place.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:give

item:
Concept: “grieving”
Statement: Whenever someone or something is grieving, they feel something very bad over one that is gone — grieving is a state they are in.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:grieving

item:
Concept: “has part (X has part Y)”
Statement: In every case of “has part (X has part Y)” as described above, X is a whole — something with parts; it can be a thing, a person, a place, or even a happening.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:has-part

item:
Concept: “has part (X has part Y)”
Statement: In every case of “has part (X has part Y)” as described above, Y is a part — something that belongs to X and helps make it up; it can be a thing, a person, a place, or even a happening.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:has-part

item:
Concept: “help (X helps Y)”
Statement: In every case of “help (X helps Y)” as described above, X is what does the helping — someone or something that makes things better or easier for Y.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:help

item:
Concept: “help (X helps Y)”
Statement: In every case of “help (X helps Y)” as described above, Y is the one helped — someone or something whose doing or situation the help is for.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:help

item:
Concept: “inside (X is inside Y)”
Statement: In every case of “inside (X is inside Y)” as described above, X is something with a place — a thing, a person, or a stuff that is somewhere; not a time.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:inside

item:
Concept: “inside (X is inside Y)”
Statement: In every case of “inside (X is inside Y)” as described above, Y is something with an inside — a thing, a place, or a container that can hold or surround; not a time.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:inside

item:
Concept: “kind (gufo:Kind, sortal type)”
Statement: In every case of a kind, there are — or could be — things of that kind: a kind is a way things are grouped as being alike.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:kind

item:
Concept: “learn (X learns Y)”
Statement: In every case of “learn (X learns Y)” as described above, X is the learner — someone or something that can come to know; not a time, a place, or a mere thing.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:learn

item:
Concept: “learn (X learns Y)”
Statement: In every case of “learn (X learns Y)” as described above, Y is what is learned — something that can be known or done: a fact, a skill, a way.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:learn

item:
Concept: “lie (the words)”
Statement: Every lie is something said — made of words, spoken or written.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:lie

item:
Concept: “lie (the words)”
Statement: In every case of a lie, the words are said to someone — a lie is told.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:lie

item:
Concept: “lie (the words)”
Statement: In every case of a lie, someone tells it — there is the one whose words they are.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:lie

item:
Concept: “lose (X loses Y)”
Statement: In every case of “lose (X loses Y)” as described above, X is the one who loses — someone or something that had Y and then does not.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:lose

item:
Concept: “lose (X loses Y)”
Statement: In every case of “lose (X loses Y)” as described above, Y is what is lost — something X had, held, or kept.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:lose

item:
Concept: “lost”
Statement: Whenever someone or something is lost, being lost is how things now stand with it — it cannot be found by, or does not know its way to, those it matters to.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:lost

item:
Concept: “make (X makes Y)”
Statement: In every case of “make (X makes Y)” as described above, X is the maker — what brings Y into being; someone or something.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:make

item:
Concept: “make (X makes Y)”
Statement: In every case of “make (X makes Y)” as described above, Y is what is made — something that comes to be through the making.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:make

item:
Concept: “make (X makes Y)”
Statement: In every case of “make (X makes Y)” as described above, the making happens at some time — there is a time at which it takes place.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:make

item:
Concept: “maker of (X is the maker of Y)”
Statement: In every case of “maker of (X is the maker of Y)” as described above, X is the maker of Y — the one that brought Y into being.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:maker-of

item:
Concept: “maker of (X is the maker of Y)”
Statement: In every case of “maker of (X is the maker of Y)” as described above, Y is what was made — something that came to be through X’s making.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:maker-of

item:
Concept: “maker of (X is the maker of Y)”
Statement: In every case of “maker of (X is the maker of Y)” as described above, a making happened — Y came to be through it, at some time.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:maker-of

item:
Concept: “memory (a stored something)”
Statement: In every case of a memory, the memory is of something — there is something it keeps or is about.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:memory

item:
Concept: “near (X is near Y)”
Statement: In every case of “near (X is near Y)” as described above, X is something with a place — a thing, a person, or a place itself; not a time.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:near

item:
Concept: “near (X is near Y)”
Statement: In every case of “near (X is near Y)” as described above, Y is something with a place — a thing, a person, or a place itself; not a time.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:near

item:
Concept: “part of (X is part of Y)”
Statement: In every case of “part of (X is part of Y)” as described above, X is a part — something that belongs to Y and helps make it up; it can be a thing, a person, a place, or even a happening.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:part-of

item:
Concept: “part of (X is part of Y)”
Statement: In every case of “part of (X is part of Y)” as described above, Y is a whole — something the part belongs to; it can be a thing, a person, a place, or even a happening.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:part-of

item:
Concept: “promise (the words)”
Statement: Every promise is something said — made of words, spoken, written, or clearly signalled.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:promise

item:
Concept: “promise (the words)”
Statement: In every case of a promise, it is made to someone — there is one to whom the word is given.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:promise

item:
Concept: “promise (the words)”
Statement: In every case of a promise, someone makes it — there is the one who gives their word.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:promise

item:
Concept: “remember (X remembers Y)”
Statement: In every case of “remember (X remembers Y)” as described above, X is the one who remembers — someone, or some being, in whose mind Y is kept or comes back; not a time, a place, or a mere thing.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:remember

item:
Concept: “remember (X remembers Y)”
Statement: In every case of “remember (X remembers Y)” as described above, Y is what is remembered — something that can be held in mind: a fact, a happening, a person, a place, a thing to do.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:remember

item:
Concept: “reminder (the made something)”
Statement: In every case of a reminder, it was made or set up — something was done so that someone will call something back to mind.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:reminder

item:
Concept: “reminder (the made something)”
Statement: In every case of a reminder, there is something it is meant to bring back to mind — the reminder is about, or for, something else.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:reminder

item:
Concept: “repair (X repairs Y)”
Statement: In every case of “repair (X repairs Y)” as described above, X is the one that does the repairing — someone or something that works on Y so that it is right again.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:repair

item:
Concept: “repair (X repairs Y)”
Statement: In every case of “repair (X repairs Y)” as described above, Y is what is repaired — a thing that was working or whole, then was not, and is worked on to be so again.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:repair

item:
Concept: “repair (X repairs Y)”
Statement: In every case of “repair (X repairs Y)” as described above, the repairing happens at some time — there is a time at which it takes place.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:repair

item:
Concept: “take (X takes Y)”
Statement: In every case of “take (X takes Y)” as described above, X is the taker — the one that comes to have or hold Y; someone or something that can do so.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:take

item:
Concept: “take (X takes Y)”
Statement: In every case of “take (X takes Y)” as described above, Y is what is taken — something that can be had, held, or carried off.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:take

item:
Concept: “take (X takes Y)”
Statement: In every case of “take (X takes Y)” as described above, the taking happens at some time — there is a time at which it takes place.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:take
